scope: GA4 web tracking-plan scenario library for human analysis and implementation planning.
source_priority: Official Google documentation first. Trusted practitioner sources only for custom-event patterns and implementation judgement.
value_convention: For controlled analytics values, use lowercase ASCII snake_case, remove accents/diacritics, and list finite options with pipes. Preserve official IDs, ISO codes, numeric values, URLs, and raw native/user-entered fields only when required and privacy-safe.

sources.name: GA4 automatically collected events
sources.used_for: Automatic and enhanced-measurement web events and default parameters.

sources.name: GA4 recommended events
sources.used_for: Full recommended event catalog and event-level parameters.

sources.name: GA4 ecommerce measurement
sources.used_for: Ecommerce dataLayer/gtag examples and items array structure.

sources.name: GA4 event parameters
sources.used_for: Parameter reporting behavior for automatic, enhanced, and recommended events.

sources.name: GA4 event setup
sources.used_for: How recommended/custom events are configured with gtag.js or GTM.

sources.name: GTM data layer
sources.used_for: dataLayer purpose and dataLayer.push implementation rules.

sources.name: GA4 custom events
sources.used_for: Rule to prefer automatic/enhanced/recommended events before custom events.

sources.name: GA4 event collection limits
sources.used_for: Event naming length and event-parameter limits.

sources.name: Simo Ahava GA4 ecommerce guide
sources.used_for: Implementation judgement for GA4 ecommerce with GTM.

sources.name: Simo Ahava GA4 events implementation guide
sources.used_for: Custom-event design judgement and GA4 implementation considerations.

sources.used_for: Typical GTM custom-event patterns and reporting considerations.

sources.name: Analytics Mania GTM data layer tutorial
sources.used_for: Human explanation and practical dataLayer examples.

standard_events.implementation: Usually automatic. Manually control only when SPA/infinite-scroll behavior requires it.
standard_events.description: This event signifies that the page loaded or the active site changed the browser history state.
standard_events.trigger: Each time the page loads or the browser history state is changed by the active site.
standard_events.official_trigger: each time the page loads or the browser history state is changed by the active site
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Page views > page_view

standard_events.description: This event signifies the first time a user visits a website with Analytics enabled.
standard_events.trigger: The first time a user visits a website with Analytics enabled.
standard_events.official_trigger: the first time a user visits a website or launches an Android instant app with Analytics enabled
standard_events.parameters: client_id, ga_session_id, ga_session_number, ignore_referrer, page_location, page_referrer, page_title, traffic_type

standard_events.description: This event signifies that a user engaged with the website and GA4 started a session.
standard_events.trigger: When a user engages the website and GA4 starts a session.
standard_events.official_trigger: when a user engages the app or website
standard_events.parameters: client_id, ga_session_id, ga_session_number, ignore_referrer, page_location, page_referrer, page_title, traffic_type

standard_events.description: This event signifies that the webpage was in focus for at least one second.
standard_events.trigger: When the webpage is in focus for at least one second.
standard_events.official_trigger: when the app is in the foreground or webpage is in focus for at least one second

standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Use a custom scroll-depth event only when additional thresholds answer an analysis need.
standard_events.description: This event signifies the first time a user reaches the bottom of a page, when 90% vertical depth becomes visible.
standard_events.trigger: The first time a user reaches the bottom of each page, when 90% vertical depth becomes visible.
standard_events.official_trigger: the first time a user reaches the bottom of each page (i.e., when a 90% vertical depth becomes visible)
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Scrolls > scroll

standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Avoid duplicating it with a custom outbound-click event.
standard_events.description: This event signifies that a user clicked a link leading away from the current domain to another website.
standard_events.trigger: Each time a user clicks a link that leads away from the current domain to another website.
standard_events.official_trigger: each time a user clicks a link that leads away from the current domain and to another website
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Outbound clicks > click

standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Optional configured query keys are emitted as q_<key>; use recommended search when explicit submitted-search measurement is also needed.
standard_events.description: This event signifies that a user was presented with a search results page identified by a configured URL query parameter.
standard_events.trigger: Each time a user is presented with a search results page identified by a configured URL query parameter.
standard_events.official_trigger: each time a user is presented with a search results page, as indicated by the presence of a URL query parameter
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Site search > view_search_results

standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement for embedded YouTube videos with JavaScript API support.
standard_events.description: This event signifies that a supported embedded YouTube video started playing.
standard_events.trigger: When an embedded YouTube video with JavaScript API support starts playing.
standard_events.parameters: video_current_time, video_duration, video_percent, video_provider, video_title, video_url, visible
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Video engagement > video_start

standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement for embedded YouTube videos with JavaScript API support.
standard_events.description: This event signifies that a supported embedded YouTube video passed 10%, 25%, 50%, or 75% of its duration.
standard_events.trigger: When an embedded YouTube video with JavaScript API support passes 10%, 25%, 50%, or 75% of its duration.
standard_events.official_trigger: when the video progresses past 10%, 25%, 50%, and 75% duration time
standard_events.parameters: video_current_time, video_duration, video_percent, video_provider, video_title, video_url, visible
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Video engagement > video_progress

standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement for embedded YouTube videos with JavaScript API support.
standard_events.description: This event signifies that a supported embedded YouTube video ended.
standard_events.trigger: When an embedded YouTube video with JavaScript API support ends.
standard_events.parameters: video_current_time, video_duration, video_percent, video_provider, video_title, video_url, visible
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Video engagement > video_complete

standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Use a custom file event only for a materially different gated or classified outcome.
standard_events.description: This event signifies that a user clicked a link to a file with a common supported download extension.
standard_events.trigger: When a user clicks a link to a file whose extension matches the enhanced-measurement download list.
standard_events.official_trigger: when a user clicks a link leading to a file (with a common file extension) of the following types
standard_events.parameters: file_extension, file_name, link_classes, link_id, link_text, link_url
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > File downloads > file_download

standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement. Use custom form-step events only when the form funnel requires them.
standard_events.description: This event signifies the first time a user interacted with a form in a session.
standard_events.trigger: The first time a user interacts with a form in a session.
standard_events.official_trigger: the first time a user interacts with a form in a session
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Form interactions > form_start

standard_events.implementation: Enhanced measurement records submission, not confirmed lead success; use generate_lead only after a valid lead outcome.
standard_events.description: This event signifies that a user submitted a form.
standard_events.trigger: When the user submits a form.
standard_events.official_trigger: when the user submits a form
standard_events.source_section: Events measurement and parameters > Form interactions > form_submit

typical_custom_events.scenario: insurance, banking, telco, services quote funnels
typical_custom_events.use_when: User starts a quote flow but no lead has been generated yet.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead after a successful lead/quote submission.

typical_custom_events.scenario: multi-step lead, signup, booking, checkout-like forms
typical_custom_events.use_when: A form step is displayed and enhanced measurement is too coarse.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use form_start/form_submit when only generic form engagement is needed.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A step is completed before final submission.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead/sign_up/purchase when the final business outcome occurs.

typical_custom_events.use_when: User sees a validation or system error that affects conversion.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: No direct GA4 recommended event fits. Avoid raw error text if it may contain PII.

typical_custom_events.scenario: listing pages, search results, catalogs, real estate, jobs
typical_custom_events.use_when: A filter materially changes the displayed results.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use view_search_results/search if the interaction is a search query.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A user changes the active result sorting rule.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: No direct recommended event fits.

typical_custom_events.use_when: Client reporting separates header navigation from menus, content, and footer links.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use select_content only for actual content objects or an approved consolidated client convention.

typical_custom_events.use_when: Top-level menu choices need direct navigation reporting.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use select_item for product tiles and select_content for actual content objects.

typical_custom_events.use_when: Second-level or deeper menu choices need to be distinguished from top-level navigation.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use a consolidated client navigation convention when menu depth has no analysis value.

typical_custom_events.use_when: Footer collection, service, legal, or social navigation needs separate reporting.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use enhanced outbound click when destination-only reporting is sufficient.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A payment attempt is refused or fails before confirmed purchase.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use purchase only after confirmed order success; no official GA4 payment-failure event exists.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A newsletter subscription is confirmed and the client reports it separately from commercial leads.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead only when newsletter and other lead outcomes intentionally share one KPI and owner.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A contact request is confirmed and needs reporting separate from other lead outcomes.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead when the contact submission is intentionally governed as the same lead KPI.

typical_custom_events.scenario: retail, publishing, travel, and mailed catalogue acquisition
typical_custom_events.use_when: A catalogue request is confirmed and fulfilment or acquisition reporting is distinct.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead when catalogue and other lead outcomes intentionally share one KPI.

typical_custom_events.use_when: User clicks a contact CTA but does not submit a lead form.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead once a valid lead/contact form is submitted.

typical_custom_events.use_when: User clicks account/login entry point before authentication.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use login after successful authentication.

typical_custom_events.use_when: Order-history usage is a meaningful self-service KPI and typed page_view reporting is insufficient.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use page_view with governed page typing when it answers the same question.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A customer views an order detail and the action needs separate self-service analysis.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use page_view with governed page typing when separate event reporting is unnecessary.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A customer starts a return workflow before any refund is completed.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use refund only when the financial or item refund is completed.

typical_custom_events.use_when: The commerce backend confirms cancellation of an existing order.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use refund separately when money or items are actually refunded.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A profile update is confirmed and self-service completion is analytically useful.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Omit the event when the change has no actionable analysis need.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A governed preference change is confirmed and approved for analytics collection.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Omit the event when consent or privacy governance does not permit collection.

typical_custom_events.use_when: Password recovery completes and access-resolution analysis is needed.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use login when successful authentication alone sufficiently measures recovery.

typical_custom_events.use_when: User starts a live chat or bot conversation.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead if chat creates a qualified lead.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A user begins a business-relevant calculator or simulator.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: No direct recommended event fits.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A user completes a business-relevant calculator or simulator and sees a result.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead if result submission creates a lead.

typical_custom_events.use_when: User selects a plan before checkout/signup.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use begin_checkout/purchase if the plan selection enters a checkout.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A key product feature is used and no recommended event fits.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use select_content/share/join_group when semantics fit.

typical_custom_events.scenario: healthcare, services, local business, real estate
typical_custom_events.use_when: User starts an appointment booking flow.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead when appointment request is submitted.

typical_custom_events.use_when: An appointment booking is successfully confirmed for the user.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use generate_lead if booked appointment is modeled as a lead.

typical_custom_events.use_when: A modal materially changes the journey or blocks/provides conversion path.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use select_content if it is simply content selection.

typical_custom_events.use_when: FAQ interaction is a real support/reassurance KPI.
typical_custom_events.prefer_official_if: Use select_content if treating FAQ selection as content selection is sufficient.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: media site, blog, documentation, editorial brand
scenario_playbooks.official_events: page_view, scroll, select_content, share, file_download, video_start, video_progress, video_complete
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: page_template, content_type, content_id, content_name, link_url, video_title
scenario_playbooks.notes: Prefer enhanced measurement for scroll/video/download when it is sufficient; use select_content for content card/article selections.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: insurance, banking, B2B services, SaaS demo, contact forms
scenario_playbooks.official_events: page_view, search, form_start, form_submit, generate_lead, qualify_lead, disqualify_lead, working_lead, close_convert_lead, close_unconvert_lead
scenario_playbooks.typical_custom_events: begin_quote, form_step_view, form_step_submit, form_error, newsletter_subscribe, contact_submit, catalog_request, contact_intent, calculator_start, calculator_complete
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: lead_source, form_name, form_step, product_type, quote_entry_point, cta_location
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use generate_lead only after a valid submission or qualified lead action. Prefer dedicated success events when newsletter, contact, catalogue, or other outcomes have distinct owners and reporting.

scenario_playbooks.official_events: view_item_list, select_item, view_item, add_to_wishlist, add_to_cart, remove_from_cart, view_cart, begin_checkout, add_shipping_info, add_payment_info, purchase, refund
scenario_playbooks.typical_custom_events: filter_apply, sort_apply, size_guide_open, stock_alert_signup, payment_error, view_order_history, view_order, start_return, cancel_order, update_profile, update_preferences, password_reset
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: currency, value, transaction_id, items, items[].item_id, items[].item_name, items[].item_category, items[].item_variant, items[].availability_status, items[].price, items[].quantity
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use official ecommerce event names and items array. Clear ecommerce before pushes. Use custom item availability on view_item when variant shortage matters, and on view_cart only for persistent carts with live inventory. Keep confirmed cancellation separate from official refund completion.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: customer portal, member area, order self-service, profile and preferences
scenario_playbooks.official_events: page_view, sign_up, login, search, select_content, add_to_wishlist, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, purchase, refund
scenario_playbooks.typical_custom_events: account_access_intent, view_order_history, view_order, start_return, cancel_order, update_profile, update_preferences, password_reset
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: account_section, entry_point, login_status, customer_status, order_status, return_scope, cancellation_stage
scenario_playbooks.notes: Explore the real authenticated journey with synthetic information. Propose no gated event from labels or public navigation alone, and keep order cancellation separate from refund completion.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: publisher subscription, paid membership, gated content, digital service plan
scenario_playbooks.official_events: page_view, select_content, sign_up, login, begin_checkout, add_payment_info, purchase, refund, generate_lead
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: content_id, content_type, paywall_type, subscription_offer, plan_id, billing_period, access_status, cancellation_reason
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use official commerce events when payment is transacted. Add paywall, offer, or cancellation events only when they support acquisition, conversion, or retention decisions.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: home page offers, banners, campaign modules, cross-sell modules
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: promotion_id, promotion_name, creative_name, creative_slot, items, items[].item_id, items[].item_name
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use promotion events only for real promotions/offers, not every generic content card.

scenario_playbooks.official_events: sign_up, login, tutorial_begin, tutorial_complete, join_group, share, select_content, purchase
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use login/sign_up for successful actions; use intent custom events for pre-auth clicks or plan exploration.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: site search, real estate, jobs, product/category listings
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: search_term, result_count, filter_category, filter_value, sort_type, item_list_id
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use search for submitted queries and view_search_results when results page detection via query parameter is enough.

scenario_playbooks.official_events: search, view_item_list, select_item, view_item, begin_checkout, add_payment_info, purchase, generate_lead
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: destination, dates, guests_count, item_id, item_name, value, currency, transaction_id
scenario_playbooks.notes: Model paid bookings as ecommerce when money is transacted; model appointment requests as leads.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: help center, claims, account support, chat
scenario_playbooks.typical_custom_events: contact_intent, chat_start, claim_intent, account_access_intent, view_order_history, view_order, start_return, cancel_order, update_profile, update_preferences, password_reset, support_article_helpful
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: support_topic, contact_method, entry_point, content_id, content_name, account_section, order_status, return_scope
scenario_playbooks.notes: Do not send claim descriptions, policy numbers, email, phone, or chat text to GA4.

scenario_playbooks.official_events: view_item_list, select_item, view_item, sign_up, begin_checkout, purchase, tutorial_begin, tutorial_complete
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: course_id, course_name, content_type, progress_percent, value, currency
scenario_playbooks.notes: Paid enrollment can use ecommerce. Learning engagement may need custom events.

scenario_playbooks.website_examples: web game, mobile game, gamified platform
scenario_playbooks.official_events: earn_virtual_currency, spend_virtual_currency, level_start, level_end, level_up, post_score, unlock_achievement, tutorial_begin, tutorial_complete
scenario_playbooks.primary_parameters: level_name, success, score, character, virtual_currency_name, value, achievement_id
scenario_playbooks.notes: Use official game recommended events wherever possible.

datalayer_patterns.name: Generic interaction event
datalayer_patterns.use_for: Custom interactions that are not ecommerce.
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: Custom Event trigger = custom_event_name. GA4 Event tag event name = {{Event}} or fixed event name. Map event_data.* as event parameters.

datalayer_patterns.name: Manual page_view with page
datalayer_patterns.use_for: SPA route changes or explicit pageview control.
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: Trigger on page_view. Map page.page_location/page_title/page_referrer/page_template/nav_language/nav_environment to identically named GA4 parameters when needed. Map approved user fields separately.

datalayer_patterns.name: Recommended search event
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = search. Map search_term and optional custom result_count.

datalayer_patterns.name: Lead success event
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = generate_lead. Map lead_source and business parameters. Mark as key event if appropriate.

datalayer_patterns.name: Custom quote start event
datalayer_patterns.use_for: User starts quote flow before a lead exists.
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = begin_quote. Register key dimensions only if needed for reporting.

datalayer_patterns.name: Ecommerce add_to_cart
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = add_to_cart. Map official GA4 parameters from the GTM ecommerce dataLayer object, for example currency from ecommerce.currency and items from ecommerce.items.

datalayer_patterns.name: Ecommerce purchase
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: GA4 Event name = purchase. transaction_id is required for deduplication/reporting quality. Map official GA4 parameters from the GTM ecommerce dataLayer object.

datalayer_patterns.name: Promotion impression/click
datalayer_patterns.gtm_mapping: Use view_promotion for exposure and select_promotion for click, keeping promotion IDs stable across both. Map official GA4 parameters from the GTM ecommerce dataLayer object.
